displayTitle: Near and Far Surface: Calling the Wall of Origin
kicker: ET LEVEL II // SIGNALS OF INTEREST

beats.narration: Run a bobbin down a tube and both surfaces report through one channel. Every signal must answer one question: which wall did it come from?

payload.title: NEAR AND FAR SURFACE
payload.sub: Name the wall before you size the flaw.
payload.panelLabel: ET BOBBIN // ID NEAR, OD FAR

beats.narration: Near and far are roles, not places. The near surface faces the coil; the far lies across the wall. Swap probes, they trade jobs.
Calibrate the bobbin: wobble flat, through-wall hole at forty degrees. ID signals climb toward it; OD signals rotate in from one-seventy, both converging there.

payload.heading: Phase calls the wall of origin
payload.note: WOBBLE HORIZONTAL · THROUGH-WALL ≈40° · ID CLIMBS, OD ROTATES IN, BOTH CONVERGE

beats.narration: Two currencies price every signal. Density collapses exponentially; phase lags fifty-seven degrees per standard depth. Near flaws read strong but crowded; far flaws weak.

payload.heading: Detect with one, discriminate with the other

beats.narration: Phase names the surface first; only then is amplitude judged. Equal volts after attenuation means the far flaw is bigger. Prove it on a standard.

payload.sub: Flaws at both surfaces on the reference standard — never guessed.
